message:
test(lifecycle): filesystem ↔ diag ↔ health three-way agreement (ibuuh.19)

ibuuh.19 retention-ground-truth row. Both health.db.exists /
health.state.index.exists and diag.database.exists / diag.index.exists
claim to report on-disk artifact presence. Verify those reports match
the ACTUAL filesystem — if a surface caches a stale exists=true while
the file is gone (or claims exists=false when the file is still on
disk), retention / GC operates on fiction and either deletes real
data or leaks orphaned artifacts.

New test db_and_index_surface_flags_match_actual_filesystem:
 * Runs cass diag --json + cass health --json against the same
   isolated empty HOME.
 * Extracts paths.db_path / paths.index_path, calls Path::exists() on
   them directly, and asserts all three (filesystem / diag / health)
   agree on existence for BOTH db and index.
 * Bonus: asserts both actually absent on disk so the three-way
   agreement isn't trivially satisfied by two matching lies.

Catches stale-cache bugs where a surface reports obsolete state and
retention / GC acts on it.

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix db_and_index_surface: 1/1 pass.

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.19; bead stays
open for the quarantine/GC runtime half.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
